input: A 24 year-old man was arrested for allegedly sexually assaulting and smothering a 75-year-old woman at Panamadangi in Vellore district of Tamil Nadu on in the early hours of Monday. The woman was staying in a bus stand at Panamadangi after the death of her husband five years ago. Police said the accused, <Person>, who was in an inebriated condition, stopped his two-wheeler behind the bus stand around 12.30am and tried to sexually assault the elderly woman. “The accused covered the woman’s mouth to stop her from raising alarm and tried to rape her. The woman was smothered when he tried to force himself on her,” said Katpadi police inspector <Person>. A woman, who was living opposite to the bus stand, found the youth trying to sexually assault the old woman. She alerted other neighbours.

input:
Students can also work at correctional facilities or as dispatchers. “We use radios here all the time. It sounds easy, but it’s really not,” Mr. <Person> says. “We teach them the codes, and we put them through training events… so they are really comfortable with it by the time they go and test. ...I’ve had a couple of different chiefs call me and say, ‘The reason we hired [that student] was because they already knew how to be a dispatcher.’”

Mr. <Person> says, “Every student we have is good. They want to be here. They have to apply to be here, and their school has to say yes, and we have to say yes, and they have to have good grades at their school. The kids are amazing. Every single one of them is just awesome.”

Mr. <Person> is proud of the fact that <Organization> law enforcement classes often boast of 50 percent female students, where the national average is about eight to 12 percent women in law enforcement. “To me, that’s pretty amazing because we need them in law enforcement and there’s not enough of them. It’s awesome to me to see that kind of interest.”

What has been Mr. <Person>’s proudest moment as a teacher? He can’t choose just one. Instead, he says, “What I feel good about is the students that we’ve had that have gone on and gotten jobs as police officers—we’ve had a ton of those.” With pride he continues, “[One student] won first at nationals, and he went to the sheriff’s office as a police explorer—first explorer ever to take over the explorer program. That’s kind of unheard of; it’s usually an officer at the department that runs it. He ran the explorers himself, and he ended up with his own office on the first floor of the sheriff’s office, which is kind of unheard of too. …He did a really good job. He went to work for <Organization> as a police aide and got his whole education paid for. …He’s doing great things.
label: APPROVED

input: Hey another black woman here in solidarity. And here's the truth, I love my blackness. I love my skin and my hair and my nigerian background. But Im tired. This shit can really suck. It does. Im tired of our children being killed. Our brothers and sisters and parents being killed. Being gaslit and told they must have been doing stuff. The constant smaller stuff. Knowing that every fuck up you make gets to be a reflection of your race. Every success makes you an outlier. I've always been incredibky high achieving and it hurts that knowing some of the compliments I've gotten has been because people are surprised Im fucking literate. This shit hurts over a life time. It gets better when you find solidarity with others and laugh at all the fucking bullshit because otherwise you'll cry. And hey, there's nothing wrong with cryng to, it doesnt fix shit  it damn does it feel good. I'm open to making a discord if ya'll are down for it.
label: APPROVED
